briefdescription.para: This function enables the specified interrupts in the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be enabled. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XDCFG_INT_* bits defined in xdevcfg_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the specified interrupts in the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be disabled. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XDCFG_INT_* bits defined in xdevcfg_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the enabled interrupts read from the Interrupt Mask Register.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XDCFG_INT_* constants defined in xdevcfg_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the IMR.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the interrupt status read from Interrupt Status Register.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XDCFG_INT_* constants defined in xdevcfg_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Interrupt Status register.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the specified interrupts in the Interrupt Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be cleared. Bit positions of 1 will be cleared. Bit positions of 0 will not change the previous interrupt status. This mask is formed by OR'ing XDCFG_INT_* bits which are defined in xdevcfg_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: The interrupt handler for the Device Config Interface.

detaileddescription.para: Events are signaled to upper layer for proper handling.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the handler that will be called when an event (interrupt) occurs that needs application's attention.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.
